hypothesis: If protein sequence and structure are optimized jointly in a single end-to-end discrete stochastic autoencoder using straight-through Gumbel-Softmax and composite structural losses, then the model will achieve higher sequence recovery and lower RMSD/higher TM-scores (better self-consistency) than non–end-to-end baselines on CATH 4.2, E2EData, and RFData because shared representations align sequence and geometric objectives during training.
